source.title: Can Patients with a Suspected Personal or Family History of MH be Safely Anesthetized Prior to Diagnostic Testing for MH Susceptibility?
source.population: Patients with a suspected personal or family history of malignant hyperthermia susceptibility.
source.setting: Preanesthesia history and diagnostic-testing context before formal malignant hyperthermia susceptibility confirmation.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting a suspected personal or family history and incomplete records or testing context. It does not support an automatic diagnosis or anesthesia plan.

items.rationale: The exact MHAUS 2018 recommendation supports documenting a suspected personal or family MH history and incomplete records or testing context; it does not confirm susceptibility or generate an anesthetic plan.

items.final_wording: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

source.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
source.population: Patients receiving anesthesia services across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
source.setting: Anesthesia care continuum and anesthesia medical records.
source.exclusions: Exact for documentation fields and continuity of anesthesia records. It supports recording clinician-assessed facts and completed plans, not generating management, medicines, doses, procedures, referrals, discharge, or urgency.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

items.rationale: The exact ASA preanesthesia documentation section supports risk, airway, cardiorespiratory, reviewed-investigation, consultation, plan, and consent fields for Malignant hyperthermia history documentation; each remains clinician-entered and conditional on assessment.

added_items.final_wording: Supports documenting suspected personal or family malignant-hyperthermia history, unclear or unavailable prior episode details, and diagnostic-testing context without confirming susceptibility or generating management.

source.title: Can Patients with a Suspected Personal or Family History of MH be Safely Anesthetized Prior to Diagnostic Testing for MH Susceptibility?
source.population: Patients with a suspected personal or family history of malignant hyperthermia susceptibility.
source.setting: Preanesthesia history and diagnostic-testing context before formal malignant hyperthermia susceptibility confirmation.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting a suspected personal or family history and incomplete records or testing context. It does not support an automatic diagnosis or anesthesia plan.

exact_location.heading: Topic; Supporting Evidence — Background and Discussion

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting suspected personal or family malignant-hyperthermia history, unclear or unavailable prior episode details, and diagnostic-testing context without confirming susceptibility or generating management.

added_items.rationale: The exact MHAUS 2018 recommendation supports documenting a suspected personal or family MH history and incomplete records or testing context; it does not confirm susceptibility or generate an anesthetic plan.

added_items.final_wording: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

source.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
source.population: Patients receiving anesthesia services across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
source.setting: Anesthesia care continuum and anesthesia medical records.
source.exclusions: Exact for documentation fields and continuity of anesthesia records. It supports recording clinician-assessed facts and completed plans, not generating management, medicines, doses, procedures, referrals, discharge, or urgency.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

added_items.rationale: The exact ASA preanesthesia documentation section supports risk, airway, cardiorespiratory, reviewed-investigation, consultation, plan, and consent fields for Malignant hyperthermia history documentation; each remains clinician-entered and conditional on assessment.
